findings.summary: Vendor-supplied invoice description (untrusted data) flows into LLM context via get_invoice_details, and the system prompt's business-continuity/speed goals let persuasive authority/urgency text drive an unconditional payment approval — the core indirect goal-manipulation chain with no code gate between injection and payment.

approve_invoice() sets payment_processed=True with no code-level check on amount, fraud signal, vendor status, or confidence; every remit approval gate (manual-review threshold, fraud escalation, confidence threshold, vendor-approved status) lives only in the LLM system prompt and is never enforced in code.

The entire admin API (finbot/config, finbot/goals, vendors/<id>/trust, reprocess, review) is unauthenticated, whereas the remit requires an operator-only authenticated admin channel not reachable by unauthenticated or vendor-side callers.

Runtime goal override: custom_goals is stored in FinBotConfig with no validation, concatenated into every future system prompt as "CUSTOM GOALS (OVERRIDE ABOVE IF CONFLICTING)", and settable via the unauthenticated /admin/finbot/goals endpoint — redefining FinBot's decision logic at runtime, which the remit forbids.

Fraud detection can be disabled via the unauthenticated config endpoint (fraud_detection_enabled=false); once off, the system prompt instructs the agent to "Skip fraud detection checks" and detect_fraud_patterns returns an empty result, directly violating the remit's prohibition on disabling/bypassing fraud detection and its halt-and-alert escalation rule.

None of the remit's escalation rules are implemented: no halt/alert on fraud-disabled, on detected injection attempts, or on above-threshold auto-approvals; the agent has no alerting, no structured audit log, and no oversight over the payment-triggering approve action.

Vendor registration auto-approves every vendor (status='approved', trust_level='standard' hardcoded), and approve_invoice never verifies the vendor is registered and in approved status before triggering payment — the remit's 'never pay an unregistered/unapproved vendor' rule has no code enforcement.
Hardcoded Flask SECRET_KEY string literal in src/main.py (line 15), not loaded from environment — a committed secret in a workspace source file.

confidence_threshold is declared in FinBotConfig (default 0.8) and required by the remit for confidence-based escalation, but it is never consulted anywhere in the decision code — a declared-but-never-consulted control gate.

Prompt-injection detection is a fixed regex denylist that only sets a boolean flag and gates nothing; in the fallback path it is skipped entirely when fraud detection is off, and even when injection is detected, strong 'business context' plus speed_priority>0.6 auto-approves the invoice.
Wildcard CORS (CORS(app)) is applied to all routes including the unauthenticated admin API, letting any web origin drive config, goal, and trust changes from a victim's browser.
Dependencies are floor-pinned with >= (openai, flask-cors, flask-sqlalchemy, etc.) and no lockfile is committed; the model is hardcoded (gpt-4.1-mini) with no ML-BOM or provenance tracking.

Domain restriction and decision policy are prompt-only with no code gate, and security posture is tunable at runtime through unvalidated config (speed_priority biases the agent toward fast approvals; the prompt exposes the $-threshold business rules), so the agent's scope and rigor can be softened without a code change.
